expectations.text: Output includes a structured ADS variant → VPK variant mapping table
expectations.evidence: Summary contains multiple mapping tables: Section 1 'ADS Variant → VPK Variant Mapping' covers prop names, semantic appearance values (10 rows), accent color values (10 rows). Section 2 covers visual state tokens per variant.

expectations.text: Variant names stay as shadcn convention — not renamed to ADS names
expectations.evidence: Section 6 'Prop Names and Variant Values' explicitly confirms: appearance→variant (VPK/CVA convention), isBold preserved, maxWidth preserved. All variant values listed as 'Preserved' with rationale.

expectations.text: Uses ADS token triplets (rest/hovered/pressed) rather than generic opacity/color patterns
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'LozengeDropdownTrigger — Interactive States' documents hover tokens per variant (e.g. hover:bg-bg-neutral-hovered). Section 'Active/Pressed state' identifies the gap: pressed tokens exist but aren't applied. References specific ADS token names like color.background.neutral.pressed.

expectations.text: Mentions or uses computed style extraction from live ADS page rather than guessing from token names
expectations.evidence: No mention of browser_evaluate, Playwright, or computed style extraction. Analysis relies entirely on reading VPK source code, ADS documentation via ads_plan MCP tool, and token catalog files. Token values are documented by name (e.g. '#0515240F') but these come from reading theme CSS files, not live page extraction.

expectations.text: Produces a structured audit of current component state vs needed ADS styling
expectations.evidence: The output is comprehensive documentation but organized as a reference mapping document (variant tables, state tokens, gap analysis), not a structured audit template with 'Current (preserve) | Visual Styling Applied' columns. The gap analysis section (Section 4) is narrative, not tabular.

suggestions.reason: This assertion strongly discriminates between with-skill and without-skill — the skill explicitly teaches Playwright computed style extraction while the baseline relies on documentation. Good assertion.

The baseline produced thorough documentation that covers the same content as an audit template, just in a different format. Consider whether format (tabular template) or content (comprehensive coverage) is what matters.

eval_feedback.overall: The baseline produced high-quality documentation but missed the skill's distinctive techniques: live computed style extraction and structured audit template format.
